title: Cross-Cultural Communication
description: Navigating high-context vs low-context cultures, directness, silence, and face-saving.
summary_pt_br: Culturas alto-contexto (implícito) vs baixo-contexto (explícito). Salvar a face evita constrangimento.

scenes.subtitle_en: Navigating high-context vs low-context cultures, directness, silence, and face-saving.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Culturas alto-contexto (implícito) vs baixo-contexto (explícito). Salvar a face evita constrangimento.

scenes.text_en: Navigating high-context vs low-context cultures, directness, silence, and face-saving. Focus: cross-cultural communication. In Japan, silence often signals careful consideration.
scenes.text_pt: Culturas alto-contexto (implícito) vs baixo-contexto (explícito). Salvar a face evita constrangimento. Foco: cross-cultural communication. No Japão, o silêncio sinaliza consideração cuidadosa.

scenes.title: Cross-Cultural Communication
scenes.body_en: High-context (implicit, Japan, Brazil): read between lines. Low-context (explicit, US, Germany): say it directly. Face-saving language prevents embarrassment: 'Let me think on that.'
scenes.body_pt: Culturas alto-contexto (implícito) vs baixo-contexto (explícito). Salvar a face evita constrangimento.

examples.en: In Japan, silence often signals careful consideration.
examples.pt: No Japão, o silêncio sinaliza consideração cuidadosa.

examples.en: Germans tend to be direct without intending rudeness.
examples.pt: Alemães tendem a ser diretos sem querer ser rudes.

examples.en: 'Let me think on that' is a polite way to disagree.
examples.pt: 'Deixe-me pensar' é uma forma educada de discordar.

exercise.question: Complete: "In Japan, silence often signals careful ___."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "In Japan, silence often signals careful consideration."
exercise.hint_pt_br: No Japão, o silêncio sinaliza consideração cuidadosa.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: Germans tend to be direct without intending rudeness.
I haven't heard anything about that.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "Germans tend to be direct without intending rudeness."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Alemães tendem a ser diretos sem querer ser rudes.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Cross-Cultural Communication — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre cross-cultural communication. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: You work with Germans and Japanese. What's different?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Germans are direct; Japanese read context.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How do you disagree in high-context cultures?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Soft language — 'Let me think on that.'

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Phrases that let someone retreat with dignity.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How do you bridge two teams?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Culture is half grammar, half body language.

scenes.title: The international standup
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Describe three cultural differences Zara has navigated. Use at least two culture-specific phrases.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: On a weekly call spanning three continents, Zara facilitated conversations between very different cultures.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: In Japan, silence often signals careful consideration — not disinterest or disagreement with what's been said.

paragraphs.speaker: Colleague
paragraphs.en: Germans, in my experience, value directness so much that softening can actually feel suspicious to them.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: 'Let me think on that' lets a Japanese colleague decline without breaking the social harmony of the room.

paragraphs.speaker: Colleague
paragraphs.en: And Americans will often say 'interesting' when they mean politely that they disagree completely.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Cross-cultural fluency, Zara discovered, was never about vocabulary — it was about listening for the sentence beneath the sentence.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Cross-Cultural Communication". Navigating high-context vs low-context cultures, directness, silence, and face-saving. In Japan, silence often signals careful consideration. Germans tend to be direct without intending rudeness. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Cross-Cultural Communication". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
